name: find: match count + the editor scrolls to a far-down match
comment: Verifies the find bar's match-count label AND that Find Next scrolls the EDITOR view to a match below the fold. Builds a 40-line document: line 1 has 'aa AA end' (two case-insensitive matches for the count check) and line 40 is a unique BOTTOMMARKER. happyPath: send the caret to the top (assert positionLabel = Ln 1), open Find, type 'aa', assert findStatusLabel reflects the match count. integrationSuite: search BOTTOMMARKER then press Find Next (cmd+g) and assert positionLabel reaches Ln 40 — proving find navigated off Ln 1 AND selectMatch scrolled the view to reveal the match (selectMatch calls scrollRangeToVisible). NOTE on medit's design: typing in the find field does a LIVE match-count but does NOT move the caret; only Find Next (cmd+g) / the chevrons advance and scroll — so the query is typed, then cmd+g is sent. (Find-scroll in the rendered PREVIEW is a separate known bug guarded by preview-find-scroll.json.) Documented primitives only; requires the Debug build.

steps.comment: Line 1 = 'aa AA end' (2 case-insensitive 'aa' matches); 38 numbered filler lines; line 40 = BOTTOMMARKER below the fold.
